page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at 2.7 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and contains no hardware GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m8gn.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test, Geekbench multi-core score, and Passmark CPU physics test all rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 21326.99 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 483.72 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top tier, with Redis achieving 357.24 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average tier. Additionally, LLM inference speed for prompt processing on a small 135M model is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and exceptional multi-threaded performance per unit of power, making it highly efficient for scaled-out workloads. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached storage due to the absence of local NVMe drives, alongside the lack of GPU acceleration. This server is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, including large-scale database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz, it is configured with 768.0 GB of system memory and a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, running on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark evaluations, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high database throughput, though single-core execution and small-model LLM prompt processing remain average to weak. This resource profile offers high multi-threaded efficiency for scaled-out deployments. It is suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput static web serving, and parallelized data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 192 dedicated Graviton cores, 768 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.48xlarge is a 192-core ARM64 general-purpose instance with 768 GB RAM and 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated cores, massive memory bandwidth, and 300 Gbps baseline networking for highly parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARM64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores operating at 2.7 GHz
768.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
High-speed networking with a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
No local storage or GPU acceleration, relying on network-attached storage
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
